title: 'Our infrastructure was designed for a climate that no longer exists': France's unfinished reckoning with increasing heatwaves
subhed: In 2003, an unprecedented heatwave struck France in the middle of August while the government was on vacation. It killed 15,000 people. While this public health shock transformed the alert system and the management of heat-related illnesses, France has failed to learn any lessons regarding its necessary adaptation to climate change.

'Summer and extreme heat make all the symptoms worse': Coping with a heatwave and mental health conditions
subhed: As temperatures rise, several studies have shown an increase in visits to psychiatric emergency rooms and suicide attempts.

title: Martin Margiela puts 200 pieces from his personal archives up for sale
subhed: On July 9 in Paris, personal items and prototypes from the famously secretive designer will be auctioned. By personally initiating and organizing the sale, Martin Margiela, who left the fashion world in 2008, is offering a glimpse into his creative process. It is a radical gesture that mirrors his artistic journey.

title: 'Is the Gulf Persian or Arabian? Or both?'
subhed: The historical designation of 'Persian Gulf' has faced challenges from Arab regimes, but in 2025, Donald Trump abandoned efforts to replace it with 'Arabian Gulf.'

title: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
subhed: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.

In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
subhed: 'Donbas: War of Attrition' (2/2). In this industrial region, 80% of which is occupied by Russian troops, the cities of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk have become the heart of the Ukrainian defensive system. Some of their residents choose to flee to safer areas. Others try to survive as best they can.

title: Parents outraged by lack of school guidelines during heatwave: 'We've had to scramble to make childcare arrangements'
subhed: As classroom conditions worsen, parents have tried to adapt by working from home, taking time off or relying on other adults for help. All have criticized the authorities' lack of preparation and have expressed concern for the years ahead.

title: Heatwave fuels frustration in Paris's northern suburbs: 'If they'd just given us shutters, we wouldn't be suffering like this'
subhed: Over the past week, daily life in two large concrete housing complexes in Seine-Saint-Denis has changed dramatically due to extreme heat. Residents share their struggles and anger as they try to help each other cope.
